meta_title: For Students: content creation to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to content creation and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Content Creation to Solve Debugging (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stuck debugging? Transform your process by documenting your journey. Use this Intermediate Checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to master your build.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at cryptic stack traces. By documenting your debugging process as technical content, you externalize your logic, forcing clarity. Use Antigravity to visualize dependencies and Claude Code to automate root-cause analysis. This shift turns frustrating blockers into high-value portfolio assets that prove your engineering competence to future employers.
content.intro:
The screen flickers with a red wall of text—another silent, impenetrable error that stalls your progress. You aren't just stuck; you're experiencing the rite of passage that separates the code-copyists from the true architects. Debugging isn't a failure; it is the most critical data point in your evolution.

By pivoting your mindset from 'fix it fast' to 'document the resolution,' you transform your terminal frustration into a blueprint for your career. This guide leverages the precision of Antigravity and the intelligence of Claude Code to turn your hardest bugs into your most compelling technical content.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure State
steps.description: Before you fix, you must define the scope. Document exactly where the state diverges from your expectation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current execution flow and identify the specific function or module where the state deviates from the initial requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'find the root cause of the current runtime error in the main loop'

steps.title: Map the Dependency Tree
steps.description: Visualize how your components interact to pinpoint which dependency is introducing the side effect.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a dependency map of the current module to visualize data flow and identify potential circular references.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'map all function dependencies for the authentication module'

steps.title: Construct the 'Bug Report' Draft
steps.description: Write a concise summary of the bug. This is the foundation of your technical content.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the technical constraints of the bug and the logical conflict discovered for a developer blog post.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'write a technical summary of the recent bug fix for a developer audience'

steps.title: Automate the Remediation
steps.description: Use AI to suggest the fix based on your documentation, ensuring your code remains clean and maintainable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactored code block that resolves the identified bug while maintaining existing interface compatibility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply the suggested fix to the codebase and run tests'

steps.title: Publish and Verify
steps.description: Finalize your technical post by documenting the solution, solidifying your understanding and building your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a 'Lessons Learned' section for this bug, highlighting the debugging methodology used for a public technical article.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'verify all unit tests pass after the fix'

pro_tips: Treat every error message as a prompt for a new blog post; if you are confused, a thousand other students are too.
Use Claude Code to generate 'why' documentation, not just code, to demonstrate your senior-level engineering thought process.
Never delete your broken code snippets; keep a 'Graveyard' folder in your repo to show employers how you iterate.
Prioritize explaining the 'logical trap' you fell into rather than just the syntax fix.
Automate your content pipeline by pushing your bug notes to a Markdown file in your repo every time you encounter a blocker.
content.next_steps: Once you've successfully debugged your first module, publish your findings on a platform like Dev.to or your personal portfolio to build social proof.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are an engineer who builds in public. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
